titleStmt.title: Memorandum by the Chairman of the State-War-Navy Coordinating Committee ( Dunn ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: Memorandum by the Chairman of the State-War-Navy Coordinating Committee ( Dunn ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Reference is made to your memorandum dated 27 November 1945,Not printed. circulated as SWNCC 159/9, in which it is proposed that United States naval personnel be withdrawn from Jan Mayen Island.
This matter was referred to the Joint Chiefs of Staff for their comments from a military point of view.

It is the opinion of the Joint Chiefs of Staff that there is no military reason for the continuance of a United States military force on that island and they therefore perceive no objection to its withdrawal. They wish to point out, however, that while withdrawal can probably take place promptly if undertaken prior to March, weather and ice conditions may cause considerable delay after that time.

The Secretary of WarRobert P. Patterson. and Secretary of the NavyJames V. Forrestal. have advised the Secretariat, State-War-Navy Coordinating Committee, that they are in accord with the views of the Secretary of State and the Joint Chiefs of Staff on this subject.
div.closer:
For the State-War-Navy Coordinating Committee:
James Clement Dunn
